title: Distributor Symphonic launches AI-powered release-plan tool - Music Ally
text:
Symphonic is the latest music distributor rolling out AI-powered features for its artist and label clients. Its latest is an ‘AI Release Marketing Plan Generator’.
It involves inputting a budget and release goals to spawn a plan for pre-save campaigns, landing pages, social posts and visual assets, ad-budget allocations and key messaging – as long as the release is at least six weeks away.
This is part of a wider trend. We’ve written about startup SymphonyOS (no relation) and its
Maestro tool
, which has been trained on hundreds of thousands of music campaigns, and which has been licensed to several distributors.
Startups and tools including
Mngrs·ai
,
Smart Music Manager
and
Venice Music’s ‘Co-Manager’
also tap AI for release plans and marketing advice, while
recent Music Ally SI:X winner Plates
provides recommendations once campaigns are underway.
For Symphonic, the new tool is part of its wider moves around AI. It has just hired two AI business analysts who’ll be scoping out new use-cases, while the company says it is also “in the process of activating key AI licensing deals and partnerships” which its clients will be able to opt in to.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
